items.label: Complainant Type
items.short_help: Identifies who brought the concern to the Ombudsman program.
items.details: Use this to clarify whether the contact came from the resident, a family member or representative, facility staff, an anonymous complainant, or another person. This helps preserve resident direction and distinguish the source of the complaint from the resident's own preferences.

items.label: Identity Permission
items.short_help: Records whether the resident or complainant permits disclosure of identifying information.
items.details: Ombudsman work is directed by resident consent and confidentiality. Document whether permission was granted, not granted, or not yet discussed before sharing identifying information with a facility or other party.

items.label: Problem Description
items.short_help: Summarizes the complaint or concern using observable facts.
items.details: Capture what was reported, who was affected, when it occurred if known, and the resident impact. Avoid legal conclusions or violation findings in the draft note.

items.label: Prior Steps Taken
items.short_help: Documents what the resident or complainant already tried before or during Ombudsman involvement.
items.details: Note whether the resident, representative, or complainant already raised the concern with staff, filed a grievance, requested a meeting, or chose not to take prior action.

items.label: Authorization Note
items.short_help: Documents consent, direction, and the requested Ombudsman role.
items.details: Use this field to record permission to act, permission to disclose identity or details, resident direction, or limits on what the Ombudsman should do. If authorization is not yet discussed, document that clearly.

items.label: Resident Perspective
items.short_help: Captures the resident's own words, preferences, and experience.
items.details: When available, document the resident's view separately from the complainant's report. This supports resident-directed advocacy and helps distinguish what the resident wants from what others request.

items.label: Resident Desired Outcome
items.short_help: Records what the resident wants to happen.
items.details: The desired outcome should reflect the resident's stated goal when known. If the resident's desired outcome is unknown or differs from the complainant's request, document that distinction.

items.label: Initial Plan of Action
items.short_help: Summarizes planned Ombudsman steps.
items.details: Examples include resident interview, facility contact with consent, records review when authorized, observation, follow-up contact, referral discussion, or monitoring. Keep the language draft-only and informational.

items.label: Actions Taken
items.short_help: Documents Ombudsman activities completed on the case.
items.details: Record concrete actions such as interviews, calls, visits, review of information, discussion of options, or follow-up. Avoid implying enforcement findings.

items.label: Facility Response
items.short_help: Summarizes what the facility reported or agreed to do.
items.details: Use neutral attribution such as 'facility stated' or 'staff reported.' Include only the response provided; do not treat it as verified unless the case note supports that.

items.label: Follow-up
items.short_help: Notes planned next contact, monitoring, or additional review.
items.details: Use this for next steps, follow-up dates, monitoring plans, or unresolved items. Follow-up can be appropriate even when a case is partially resolved or awaiting resident confirmation.

items.label: Status or Outcome
items.short_help: Describes the current case status, outcome, or resolution narrative.
items.details: Document the current state of the issue and whether the resident's goal was met, partially met, not met, or still pending when known. Keep this separate from legal or regulatory conclusions.

items.label: Case Status
items.short_help: Indicates whether the case is open, partially resolved, resolved, or closed.
items.details: Use this to reflect case workflow status. A closed case should have enough outcome information for review, but closure does not mean the tool has determined a violation or legal result.

items.label: Resident Satisfaction
items.short_help: Records the resident's satisfaction with the outcome when known.
items.details: Resident satisfaction should reflect the resident's view, not the facility's or complainant's view. If the resident has not been asked or satisfaction is not documented, use that option.

items.label: Checklist Review
items.short_help: Shows whether common case-note elements are present, missing, or unclear.
items.details: This is an internal drafting aid. Missing items are not automatically errors unless the validation rules separately flag them.

items.label: Validation Results
items.short_help: Flags draft-note fields that may need review before use.
items.details: Validation helps identify missing or warning fields. It does not decide whether a complaint is substantiated, whether a violation occurred, or whether enforcement action is appropriate.

items.label: Likely Topics
items.short_help: Shows topics inferred from selected NORS code or text keywords.
items.details: Topic matches help organize reference support. They are not complaint determinations and should be reviewed by staff.

items.label: Likely NORS Codes
items.short_help: Shows possible NORS code matches for review.
items.details: Likely NORS codes are screening suggestions based on the selected code and/or matched concern text. Confirm the final code against the Table 2 definition, examples, and reporting tips.
items.source: NORS Table 1 Case Level 2024 and NORS Table 2 complaint code guidance

items.label: Possible Relevant References
items.short_help: Lists authorities connected to matched topics or NORS codes.
items.details: References are research leads for review and drafting. They are not findings that a violation occurred and do not determine legal conclusions or required actions.

items.label: Handouts & Resources
items.short_help: Shows educational resources related to the selected code or matched concern.
items.details: Resources may include NORS guidance, resident-facing handouts, or official reference materials. They are for education and review support only and do not decide the final code or outcome.
items.source: App guidance using NORS Table 2 and official resource links

items.label: Crosswalk Trace
items.short_help: Explains why topics and references were surfaced.
items.details: The trace shows which NORS code or keyword text triggered each topic, likely code, and authority reference. Use it to see where human review is still needed.

items.label: Keyword / Concern
items.short_help: Free-text concern used to infer likely topics.
items.details: Use plain language such as 'call light not answered' or 'discharge notice.' Keyword lookup is a screening aid and should be reviewed against the case facts.
